headline: Walmart’s (NASDAQ:WMT) Q1 CY2026 Earnings Results: Revenue In Line With Expectations
summary: Retail behemoth Walmart (NASDAQ:WMT) met Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 6.1% year on year to $175.7 billion. On the other hand, next quarter’s revenue guidance of $185.4 billion was less impressive, coming in 0.5% below analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.66 per share was in line with analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Walmart’s (NASDAQ:WMT) Q1 CY2026 Earnings Results: Revenue In Line With Expectations
content.summary: Retail behemoth Walmart (NASDAQ:WMT) met Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 6.1% year on year to $175.7 billion. On the other hand, next quarter’s revenue guidance of $185.4 billion was less impressive, coming in 0.5% below analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.66 per share was in line with analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Walmart delivers another strong quarter but also a cautious outlook due to economic uncertainty
summary: Walmart delivered another quarter of impressive sales as speedy deliveries and low prices served as a magnet for shoppers across the income spectrum.  Walmart has resonated with many Americans who are increasingly careful about where they spend their money as inflation has taken a bigger bite out of paychecks, particularly since the start of the Iran war in late February.  Traffic at Walmart can be a barometer of consumer spending given its vast customer base.

headline: Is Walmart stock beginning to crack under stretched consumers?
summary: Walmart (WMT) is in focus on Thursday after the chain retailer's adjusted earnings ($0.66 per share) and revenue ($177.8 billion) came in line with Wall Street estimates. The stock is dropping after maintaining its full-year outlook and reporting a 4.1% increase in US same-store sales. Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi leads a panel discussion with Senior Markets Reporter Ines Ferré, Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre, and Freedom Capital Markets managing director Paul Meeks over whether Walmart should be worried about pressured consumers and the store's second quarter outlook.

content.title: Is Walmart stock beginning to crack under stretched consumers?
content.description:
Walmart (WMT) is in focus on Thursday after the chain retailer's adjusted earnings ($0.66 per share) and revenue ($177.8 billion) came in line with Wall Street estimates. The stock is dropping after maintaining its full-year outlook and reporting a 4.1% increase in US same-store sales.

Yahoo Finance Executive Editor Brian Sozzi leads a panel discussion with Senior Markets Reporter Ines Ferré, Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre, and Freedom Capital Markets managing director Paul Meeks over whether Walmart should be worried about pressured consumers and the store's second quarter outlook.
content.summary: Walmart (WMT) is in focus on Thursday after the chain retailer's adjusted earnings ($0.66 per share) and revenue ($177.8 billion) came in line with Wall Street estimates. The stock is dropping after maintaining its full-year outlook and reporting a 4.1% increase in US same-store sales. headline: Walmart wins over broader swath of consumers, but global uncertainty clouds outlook for retailers
summary: Walmart delivered another quarter of impressive sales with speedy deliveries and low prices becoming a strong magnet for people across the income spectrum that are spending more on almost everything, particularly gasoline.  Walmart has resonated with many Americans who are increasingly careful about where they spend their money as inflation takes a bigger bite out of paychecks, notably gasoline which has soared since the start of the Iran war in late February.  Walmart can serve as a barometer of consumer spending given its vast customer base.

content.title: Walmart wins over broader swath of consumers, but global uncertainty clouds outlook for retailers
content.summary: Walmart delivered another quarter of impressive sales with speedy deliveries and low prices becoming a strong magnet for people across the income spectrum that are spending more on almost everything, particularly gasoline.  Walmart has resonated with many Americans who are increasingly careful about where they spend their money as inflation takes a bigger bite out of paychecks, notably gasoline which has soared since the start of the Iran war in late February.  Walmart can serve as a barometer of consumer spending given its vast customer base.

headline: The 3 core themes in Walmart's earnings. One of them is AI.
summary: Walmart (WMT) stock has taken a dip on its first quarter results that were in line with Wall Street estimates, reporting $177.8 billion in revenue and adjusted earnings of $0.66 per share. The retailer also reported 4.1% in US same-store sales growth and maintained its full-year outlook. TD Cowen senior research analyst Oliver Chen reacts to Walmart's figures and explains how he is viewing value themes overtake the retail sector as consumers remain hard pressed by higher prices.

content.title: The 3 core themes in Walmart's earnings. One of them is AI.
content.description:
Walmart (WMT) stock has taken a dip on its first quarter results that were in line with Wall Street estimates, reporting $177.8 billion in revenue and adjusted earnings of $0.66 per share. The retailer also reported 4.1% in US same-store sales growth and maintained its full-year outlook.

TD Cowen senior research analyst Oliver Chen reacts to Walmart's figures and explains how he is viewing value themes overtake the retail sector as consumers remain hard pressed by higher prices.
content.summary: Walmart (WMT) stock has taken a dip on its first quarter results that were in line with Wall Street estimates, reporting $177.8 billion in revenue and adjusted earnings of $0.66 per share. The retailer also reported 4.1% in US same-store sales growth and maintained its full-year outlook. TD Cowen senior research analyst Oliver Chen reacts to Walmart's figures and explains how he is viewing value themes overtake the retail sector as consumers remain hard pressed by higher prices.

content.summary: The retailer’s price reductions now reach 7,200 products — but high fuel prices could eventually put pressure on those investments.

thumbnail.caption: A Walmart store is seen on Feb. 3, 2026 in Austin, Texas. The company credited AI with boosting customer spend and improving inventory management, executives said Thursday during an earnings call.
